title: Podman-remote 6+ non-functional inside a Flatpak sandbox
body:
### Issue Description

`podman-remote` 6+ crashes when run inside a Flatpak sandbox. I'm using it to access the host's Podman engine.

The root cause seems to be the check added in https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/commit/e860773c0d9d3a9cbe8637ed459bf7e68e257a57 which crashes the CLI when Cgroups v2 is not mounted locally. The Flatpak sandbox does not mount `/sys/fs/cgroup` ([cite](https://docs.flatpak.org/en/latest/sandbox-permissions.html#reserved-paths)).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```bash
flatpak run --filesystem=host --filesystem=xdg-run/podman \
  --command=$(realpath .../podman-remote) <any Flatpak app> ps
```

Or, using an explicit `bwrap` command:
```bash
bwrap --ro-bind "$HOME" "$HOME" \
  --bind /run /run \
  --proc /proc --dev /dev \
  --unshare-all --die-with-parent \
  "$HOME"/.../podman-remote ps
```


### Describe the results you received

```
FATA[0000] Error determining cgroups mode
```

### Describe the results you expected

The same output as a normal `podman ps`:
```
$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
...
```

Or an error message of the form:
```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v6.0.1/libpod/_ping": dial unix /run/user/.../podman/podman.sock: connect: connection refused
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

As a workaround, adding the permission `flatpak run --filesystem=/sys/fs/cgroup:ro ...` is sufficient to make `podman-remote` work again.
